body_txt: Capping or replacing teeth with gold is a common dental procedure. Because of how conspicuous gold is , especially compared to less noticeable types of dental prostheses, fictional characters who get gold teeth often do so as a status symbol and to show off their wealth . A variation are golden grills (false tooth covers), which arose in the hiphop and rap subcultures of the USA in the late 20th century. In the modern day, gold teeth are regarded as rather ostentatious and may indicate newfound wealth . Because of the various ways wealthy people can be shady, villainous, or otherwise antagonistic, it is also common for these glitzy golden gnashers to overlap with Villainous Gold Tooth . In the case of wealthy pirates with gold teeth, there's overlap with Dressed to Plunder . For using your teeth to check the purity of gold, see Tasty Gold , though there can certainly be some overlap with this trope; what better way to show how rich you are (or how much booty you've plundered) by nibbling some gold with the gold teeth in your mouth! Subtrope of Made O' Gold and Gold Makes Everything Shiny .
